role_title: Product Manager of AI Applications, Global Public Sector
secondary_archetype: Technical PM with forward-deployed / enterprise solutioning focus

level_signals: Leads cross-functional team of engineers, MLEs, and operators
Owns large AI projects for one or many customers
Drives millions in revenue for the business

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of experience building products with specific experience within the last 1-2 years building AI-powered products
Strong technical background (STEM degree) and/or experience building technical software products
Strong understanding of generative AI technologies and their applications in both enterprise and consumer settings
Experience with vibe coding tools (i.e., Replit, Lovable, Bolt, etc.) and design tools (i.e., Figma/Canva/Miro)
Exceptional leadership, presentation and communication skills with the ability to influence cross-functional teams

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proficiency in Arabic, both written and spoken

explicitly_excluded: Candidates who applied for the same role within the last 90 days are not reconsidered (90-day waiting period policy)

scope.surface: Custom AI applications and custom LLMs for governments and government-backed entities outside the United States, powered by the Scale GenAI Platform
scope.business_model: B2G (business-to-government), enterprise AI solutions with bespoke forward-deployed development
primary_metrics_inferred: Revenue generated per customer (millions in revenue cited)
AI application adoption and deployment success
core_responsibilities: Lead design workshops with the client to define custom AI solutions
Scope out new AI application use cases across various government entities
Lead cross-functional development of AI applications and custom LLMs with diverse stakeholders
Consistently engage with future end-users to solicit feedback and ensure effective prioritization
Stay up to date with latest research in applied AI and training custom LLMs
Scope out model evaluation sets and performance requirements, review results, and iterate on the solution
Give regular progress updates to the client and Global Public Sector leadership

green_flags: Clear ownership scope: large AI projects for one or many government customers
Entrepreneurial role on a rapidly growing team
Explicit mention of real-world impact: government entities and frontier AI agents
Nice-to-haves are well-scoped and not inflated into hard requirements
Equal opportunity employer with explicit disability accommodation process
red_flags: No compensation range disclosed anywhere in the JD
Location not specified — unclear whether role is remote, hybrid, or onsite, and in which country/region
Work authorization requirements not stated — role involves non-US governments but candidate geography is unclear
Arabic as a nice-to-have strongly implies MENA focus but the region is not explicitly named in the role scope
90-day re-application waiting period may disadvantage candidates who applied recently

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, cross-functional ownership, and revenue accountability; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from 'governments and government-backed entities outside the United States' and bespoke solution framing)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly listed in JD)
location.type (not stated in JD; marked unclear)
geographies_named on arabic_proficiency nice-to-have (MENA inferred from Arabic language requirement and Government of Qatar reference in About Us)
ambiguities: Location of the role is entirely unstated — the team operates globally (outside the US) but no city, country, or remote policy is mentioned for the candidate's base.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not addressed in the JD body.
STEM degree is listed as 'and/or experience building technical software products', making it partially substitutable — severity kept hard but accepts_equivalent_experience set to true.
The 'Global Public Sector' team focuses on non-US governments, but the About Us section references US government agencies (Army, Air Force) — unclear whether this role could touch US government work.
